htmlclay — icon system

One mark everywhere, rendered per context. Generated from blob.svg + blob-noshadow.svg.

Decision: the blob is the brand. Used everywhere — app, dock, menu bar, doc, favicon, website. App icon is the blob on a black squircle. The contact shadow lives on its own layer, dropped for tiles, dark backgrounds, and tray. The face sits a touch higher on the body, and small sizes (tray, favicon) use a bigger classic grin so it survives. The "CLAY" wordmark is retired from icons and lives on as the website logotype.

The mark

App icon · black squircle
Brand / website · clean mark

Contact shadow — its own layer

SHADOW ON
Standalone marklight surface · grounded
SHADOW OFF
Icon marktiles · favicon · doc · header
SHADOW OFF
On darkblack shadow would turn to mud

The shadow is a single <g id="shadow"> ellipse in blob.svg. Drop that group (or use blob-noshadow.svg) and the body keeps clean rounded feet.

Legibility ladder — survives to 16px

128
64
32
16
32px (actual pixels)
16px (actual pixels)

In the dock

Menu bar / tray — friendlier smile (5 takes)

CHOSEN
HTML Clay
9:41
HTML Clay
9:41
V1 · Classic grin
HTML Clay
9:41
HTML Clay
9:41
V2 · Line smile
HTML Clay
9:41
HTML Clay
9:41
V3 · Crescent
HTML Clay
9:41
HTML Clay
9:41
V4 · Cheek-to-cheek
HTML Clay
9:41
HTML Clay
9:41
V5 · Smile only

Chosen: V1 (classic grin) for the tray and the favicon, the small contexts where the master’s smaller smile would vanish. Everything bigger keeps the existing mark. All five cut the face out of the solid silhouette (monochrome template icon), so it inverts cleanly: black on a light bar, white on a dark bar. The smile is sized up from the master so it survives at 18 px. V1 classic, V2 a single curved line, V3 a solid crescent, V4 a wide cheek-to-cheek grin (rounded tips, sagging top lip), V5 mouth only.

Document icon & Finder

.htmlclay
weekly-planner.htmlclay
.htmlclay
recipes.htmlclay

Document icon = the clean mark on a page shape, so .htmlclay files read as "an htmlclay thing" while staying distinct from the app tile. No shadow on the page.

Browser tab (favicon)

Weekly Planner×
New Tab
127.0.0.1 · weekly-planner.htmlclay

Favicon uses the V1 grin (bigger smile) so the face still reads at 16 px. The app tile and everything larger keep the standard mark.

Website header (logotype)

htmlclay
DownloadDocsBlog

The wordmark keeps the name where there's room to read it. Same blob, set next to the type.

App-icon background — the choice

CHOSEN
Black squircle
high contrast, pops in the dock
Cream squircle
friendly, keeps the outline

Tray silhouette on light vs dark — one shape, inverts to fit the bar.

Self-contained: every image is embedded. Source: blob.svg (shadow on its own layer) and blob-noshadow.svg. Next step is dist/icons/generate.sh to emit .icns, doc.icns, Windows .ico, Linux PNG/SVG, and the tray icon.
